title: E2E tests — privacy, write-only flow, merge semantics
description: Lock down the privacy and data-merge guarantees of Sprint 016 with Playwright tests. These are regression gates — if a later change adds a read-back path or leaks a name on the token URL, this suite should fail loudly.
acceptance_criteria: Test: /my-profile/{token} rendered HTML contains initials but NOT first name, last name, or preferred name for the seeded player
Test: /my-profile/{token}/history rendered HTML contains initials only for the player and any opponents
Test: page <title> on /my-profile/{token} does not contain the player's first or last name
Test: GET /my-profile/{token} renders blank form; after POST with partial data and a second GET, the form is still blank
Test: POST with a subset of fields saves only those fields — a prior POST's other fields are not cleared (merge semantics)
Test: explicit 'clear' on a multi-select empties the list on the server
Test: confirmation page after POST shows only the just-submitted fields and does not display prior stored values
Test: /my-availability/{token} renders a visible CTA linking to /my-profile/{token}
Test: admin view on /admin/league/players/{id} shows 'My Tennis' summary with stored values (via admin session auth)
Test: player-facing pages do NOT contain a seeded captain_player_notes body (leak regression — further coverage in Sprint 017 WI-107)
All tests parameterised on the seeded fantasy token from fixtures/seed.sql and seeded admin credentials
Tests pass headlessly and at 2+ workers

files_to_modify: tests/e2e/fixtures/seed.sql — seed preferences and a captain note for regression coverage

testing_requirements: Tests are resilient to copy tweaks (assert data-testid / ARIA selectors rather than brittle text)

context.user_story: As a maintainer, I want the privacy properties of 'My Tennis' to be enforced by CI so a future change doesn't silently reopen the read-back path.
